title: Indigofera semhaensis
taxonomy: Indigofera semhaensis belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes a wide range of species, many of which are known for their ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. Indigofera semhaensis is further classified as a genus within the family, along with other species such as Indigofera tinctoria and Indigofera trifoliata. The genus Indigofera is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, and is known for its diverse range of species, many of which are used as ornamental plants or for their medicinal properties. Indigofera semhaensis is likely to be classified within the same general framework as other species within the genus.
